title: Miquel Sirera
text:
Hi, I’m Miquel
I work at the intersection of machine learning and distributed systems — building large language models that can train and run across many devices, gracefully handle failure, and stay efficient at the edge.
Before Boston, I studied Data Science at UPC in Barcelona. I like research that ships, collaborators who push my thinking, and problems that don’t have a clean answer yet. If any of what’s below sounds interesting, reach out — I’m always up for a conversation.

Distributed LLMs Resilient Inference Edge AI Knowledge Editing Wireless + ML PyTorch CUDA Python


What I’m working on

title: Miquel Sirera
text:
Current projects


Flagship

Resilience for Distributed LLMs
Extending JARVIS — a framework that splits LLM layers across edge devices with limited compute. We add peer-to-peer recovery and layer redundancy so the system keeps serving when nodes fail. Tested on Gemma-2B across 18 software-defined radios in the NSF Colosseum emulator and a 7-node Raspberry Pi cluster. More in Research.



Networking + ML

Communication-aware DNN pruning
Training networks for distributed deployment so they need less inter-device communication while staying accurate. Pruning and placing neurons in CNNs and MLPs so they run well on the edge under mixed network conditions. Builds on prior work from our group.



LLMs

Knowledge editing in LLMs
Looking at more efficient ways to update or correct specific facts and behaviors inside large language models — without full retraining, and without breaking everything else the model knows.




A bit more

title: Research

Most of my work sits at the intersection of AI, distributed systems, and wireless. Below is what’s published, where, and what I’m presenting next. If anything sparks ideas, reach out.

title: Resume
text:
CV & background

Academic CV Industry CV

ProfileEducationExperienceSide projectsSkills


I have a BSc in Data Science and Engineering from Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya, with a solid foundation in CS, math, statistics, and signal processing. I’m now doing a Ph.D. in Computer Engineering at Northeastern, focused on research that pushes things forward — especially in ML, distributed systems, and large language models. I like getting things shipped and aim for work that has real impact.

Machine Learning Large Language Models Distributed Systems Edge AI Wireless + AI PyTorch CUDA Python Linux MLOps





Jan 2024 — Present

PhD in Computer Engineering
Northeastern University · Boston
At the intersection of ML, LLMs, distributed training and inference, and edge AI. Interested in making models more resilient and robust, in running AI efficiently on edge and cloud, and in how AI can improve telecom — network performance and automation. Especially curious about LLMs: attention, knowledge representation, and editing. Goal: build systems that are efficient, scalable, and reliable.



Sep 2019 — Jul 2023

BSc in Data Science & Engineering
Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya · Barcelona
Graduated with GPA 8.89/10. Honors in four courses: Discrete Mathematics & Logic, Mathematical Optimization, Search and Analysis of Information, and Advanced Topics of Data Science.



Sep 2017 — Jun 2019

High School
La Salle Bonanova · Barcelona
Graduated with honors. Scored 13.556/14 on the PAU university entrance exams and earned the PAU distinction. Math contests (Pangea, “Fem Matemàtiques”) and extra math enrichment.







Feb 2023 — Jul 2023

BSc Thesis · Genesys Lab
Northeastern University · Institute for the Wireless Internet of Things
Worked with a research team on AI for wireless communications. Designed and implemented AI-based approaches for signal classification. This work got me into AI + wireless and I’ve been here since.



Mar 2022 — Dec 2022

AI Engineer Intern
Abi Global Health
Internship at a global tele-health company. Improved monitoring in their MLOps setup and helped the AI Lab build new ML models and improve existing ones.







2021 — Present

Datathons & hackathons
Various
Done a bunch of datathons and hackathons — UPC Datathon and Google Hash Code (twice among others). Sharp problem-solving practice and good teamwork reps.



Feb 2022 — Jun 2022

BECO · environmentally-conscious decisions
Naptilus Tech Lab
Built a software product that used user data to help people reduce their carbon footprint. Shipped “BECO” — an app that used ML to suggest more sustainable options (e.g. eco-friendly places).





I’m strong in problem-solving and critical thinking, and I work well in teams. I pick up new things quickly and I’m comfortable with change. I care about doing good work, I manage my time well, and I stay organized. That’s how I get things done.
